message:
feat(abi): port libbsd nlist over ELF64 symbol table parser (bd-lqllh)

Add nlist(3): open a binary file, parse its ELF64 section header
table, locate the first SHT_SYMTAB section + its associated SHT_STRTAB
via sh_link, then for each requested entry in the caller's nl array
scan symbol entries by name. Found symbols receive st_info / st_other
/ st_shndx / st_value mapped into n_type / n_other / n_desc / n_value;
unfound entries are zeroed.

Loop terminates on entry.n_name == NULL or *entry.n_name == '\0' (the
NetBSD-style empty-string sentinel). 65536-entry walk cap defends
against missing terminators. Returns -1 on file-open failure, ELF
header rejection, ELF32 / non-LSB inputs, missing symbol table, or
malformed section headers; otherwise returns the count of unfound
symbols.

The new module lives in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/nlist_abi.rs and
defines a #[repr(C)] CNlist matching libbsd's <nlist.h> layout (24
bytes on x86_64).

Tests: 8 abi tests (NULL path, NULL array, missing file, non-ELF
file, empty file, empty request on /proc/self/exe, find `main` +
miss bogus symbol with correct unfound count, empty-string n_name
terminator).

Closes the last libbsd parity gap reported by the abi-vs-libbsd probe.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-locl1","title":"CONFORMANCE: libc locale-aware _l function variants","description":"Differential harness shipped (see commit). 8 functions: isalpha_l/isdigit_l/isspace_l/toupper_l/tolower_l (exhaustive 0..=255 + EOF in C locale), strtol_l (11 numeric cases), strtod_l (12 cases including inf/nan/garbage), newlocale (valid C and garbage names). Discovered DISC-LOCALE-001: fl::newlocale accepts category_mask with the LC_ALL bit set; glibc rejects it. Documented; not failed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T10:00:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T10:00:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T10:00:00Z","close_reason":"Harness shipped; DISC-LOCALE-001 documented; bd-locl2 opened for fl::newlocale mask validation.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","differential","locale"]}
 {"id":"bd-locl2","title":"FIX: fl::newlocale should reject category_mask with LC_ALL bit (DISC-LOCALE-001)","description":"POSIX 2008 newlocale: category_mask must be a bitwise OR of LC_*_MASK constants. The LC_ALL bit (1<<6 = 64) is not a valid mask bit; glibc returns NULL+EINVAL when it is set. fl::newlocale currently accepts it and returns a non-null locale_t. Fix: validate category_mask & ~LC_ALL_MASK_VALID == 0 in locale_abi.rs newlocale.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T10:00:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T10:01:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T10:01:00Z","close_reason":"Reject invalid newlocale category-mask bits with EINVAL","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","conformance","locale"]}
 {"id":"bd-lpch","title":"syscall: sched_getaffinity zero cpusetsize should return EINVAL exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for sched_getaffinity(0, 0, &mask). Add focused ABI regression coverage so the zero-size contract is explicit.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:08:22.213437547Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T02:47:42.739876316Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T02:47:42.737701699Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-lqllh","title":"PORT: nlist (libbsd ELF symbol table reader)","description":"libbsd nlist(filename, struct nlist *nl): read symbol table from an ELF binary file and fill caller-supplied nlist entries. Each entry has n_un.n_name (input symbol name pointer), n_type/n_other/n_desc/n_value (output). Loop terminates when entry.n_name is NULL or *entry.n_name == 0. Returns -1 on file open / parse error, otherwise count of symbols not found. Implementation: open file readonly, mmap or read enough to parse ELF64 ehdr + section header table, locate .symtab and .strtab sections, scan Elf64_Sym entries comparing names against requested entries, fill in n_type=st_info, n_other=st_other, n_desc=st_shndx, n_value=st_value for matches. ELF32 and non-ELF inputs return -1. Layout: nlist entry is 24 bytes (union 8B + u8 + c_char + i16 + 4B pad + c_ulong) on x86_64.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T14:38:36.151160139Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T14:38:36.151160139Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-lri5x","title":"PORT: snprintb / snprintb_m (BSD libutil bit-name formatter)","description":"Port the BSD libutil snprintb(buf, bufsize, fmt, val) bit-field name printer used by netstat/vmstat/route/etc to render packed bit values with human-readable labels. Format string starts with a base character (\010=octal, \020=hex) followed by simple bit specs of the form \\<bit-number><name>\\ where bit-number is 1-based. Example: snprintb(buf, sz, "\\010\\1FOO\\2BAR", 3) -> "03<FOO,BAR>". Implementation: pure-safe core helper format_snprintb(fmt: &[u8], val: u64) -> Vec<u8> that walks the format string, emits the base-formatted value, and appends a "<name1,name2,...>" list of the names whose bit is set; thin abi shim that copies into the caller-supplied bounded buffer (NUL-terminated, returns full required length per snprintf convention). Also ship snprintb_m(buf, bufsize, fmt, val, max_per_line) which inserts a newline + restart-token between groups when the rendered text would exceed max_per_line characters. Tests: hex/octal base, multiple bits set, no bits set, oversize buffer truncation, NULL/empty fmt, snprintb_m line-splitting.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T09:00:59.708953645Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T09:06:52.708035867Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T09:06:52.706032343Z","close_reason":"shipped: snprintb + snprintb_m BSD bit-name formatter with octal/hex bases, named-bit list rendering, multi-line splitting + 12 core tests + 10 abi tests, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-lu1c","title":"syscall: memfd_secret","description":"Add raw memfd_secret syscall veneer in frankenlibc-core with focused syscall veneer tests for availability and invalid flag behavior. Intended as the next bd-h5x raw-syscall slice; this br build rejects --parent=bd-h5x as ambiguous.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T01:09:06.062393590Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T01:13:24.045830299Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T01:13:24.043823012Z","close_reason":"Added raw memfd_secret veneer in frankenlibc-core with focused syscall tests and passed rch core gates.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-luc3d","title":"printf_conformance executor ignores length modifiers (%Lf/%td/%zd/%jd); causes varargs width mismatch","description":"crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs run_host_snprintf takes PrintfArg::Int and passes c_int to libc::snprintf, but when the format specifier uses length modifiers %td (ptrdiff_t), %zd (size_t), %jd (intmax_t), %ld, %lld, glibc reads the arg at the wider width and gets garbage upper bits. Fix: when parsing args, inspect the format string for length modifiers and promote numeric args to PrintfArg::Long before dispatch. Surfaced by sprintf_t_d in bd-12hh matrix (expected '-12345', host returned '4294954951' due to stack-read overflow).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T05:32:32.945225484Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T06:07:02.859074014Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T06:07:02.856763492Z","close_reason":"promote_args_by_format() scans fmt specifiers and promotes Int->Long/Ulong where length modifier demands 64-bit. 8 integer length-modifier cases removed from KNOWN_IMPL_GAPS. %Lf/%Le remain skipped (f80 has no Rust equivalent). Committed d7ee870.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-rqn","title":"Production kernel manifest: minimal required math set + compile-time gates","description":"Critique mapping: #5.

Deliverables:
- Define default production kernel (small, explainable, benchmarked).
- Add compile-time feature gates separating production vs research modules.

Acceptance:
- Default build excludes ResearchOnly modules.
- Production kernel meets strict/hardened latency budgets.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","notes":"Perf gate rerun on idle host passed (scripts/perf_gate.sh): strict deltas within threshold, hardened decide_observe delta +1.74%. However target budget checks still rely on allow_target_violation policy in perf gate, and manifest currently keeps all runtime_math modules in production with research_only_modules empty. Bead remains in_progress pending minimal production subset selection + explicit research-only migration plan.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureBluff","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:11.222573367Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T16:39:50.848168045Z","closed_at":"2026-02-19T16:39:50.848140263Z","close_reason":"Completed minimal production kernel manifest partition (25 production / 44 research-only), synchronized governance/policy artifacts, and validated via runtime-math gates + rch harness tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","critique","math"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-rqn","depends_on_id":"bd-1iya","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-rqn","depends_on_id":"bd-1rxj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-rqn","depends_on_id":"bd-24x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-rqn","depends_on_id":"bd-25pf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-rqn","depends_on_id":"bd-2k6b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-rqn","depends_on_id":"bd-7dw2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1136,"issue_id":"bd-rqn","author":"Codex","text":"Codex here. I just claimed bd-33p.1 (canonical evidence schema v2) and will push updates there; ping me here if you need anything or see conflicts.","created_at":"2026-02-12T21:12:47Z"},{"id":1137,"issue_id":"bd-rqn","author":"Codex","text":"Claimed bd-33p.2 (trace propagation + decision explainability fields). I’ll keep changes tight and comment here if anything overlaps with your in-flight work.","created_at":"2026-02-12T21:24:18Z"},{"id":1138,"issue_id":"bd-rqn","author":"BronzePine","text":"Progress update (BronzePine): implemented bd-rqn enabling change for production/research manifest partitioning safety gates.\n\nCode changes:\n- crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/math_governance_test.rs: added manifest_union_modules() helper and switched governance coverage assertions from production-only to manifest union (production_modules U research_only_modules).\n- scripts/check_math_governance.sh: updated Check 2/3 to validate against manifest union and updated pass/fail messaging accordingly.\n\nValidation:\n- scripts/check_math_governance.sh => PASS\n- rch doctor => PASS\n- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test math_governance_test => PASS (8/8)\n- rch exec -- cargo fmt --check => PASS\n- rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets => PASS (warnings only; existing unrelated warnings in frankenlibc-abi)\n- rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings => FAIL due pre-existing unrelated lint debt in frankenlibc-abi (unused imports, unreachable code, dead_code, clippy style lints in locale_abi/stdio_abi/termios_abi/time_abi).\n\nRCH notes:\n- Remote offload attempted for heavy cargo commands; some runs failed open to local due worker sync instability (no-space/file-vanished on remote rsync).","created_at":"2026-02-15T17:37:03Z"},{"id":1139,"issue_id":"bd-rqn","author":"BronzePine","text":"Additional bd-rqn progress (BronzePine): updated scripts/runtime_math_admission_gate.py to support manifest partition semantics.\n\nDetails:\n- Manifest extraction now normalizes both  and .\n- Admission checks run over union(manifest sets), while ADMITTED status is restricted to modules in .\n- Production-core completeness now checks specifically against production set.\n- Admission ledger now emits  and  booleans in addition to .\n\nValidation:\n- python3 scripts/runtime_math_admission_gate.py => status=pass, errors=0\n- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test runtime_math_admission_gate_test => PASS (3/3)","created_at":"2026-02-15T17:41:34Z"},{"id":1140,"issue_id":"bd-rqn","author":"BronzePine","text":"Correction for prior note formatting: updated scripts/runtime_math_admission_gate.py to support manifest partition semantics. Manifest extraction now normalizes both production_modules and research_only_modules; admission checks run over the union while ADMITTED status is restricted to production_modules. Ledger now includes in_production_manifest and in_research_only_manifest booleans. Validation: python3 scripts/runtime_math_admission_gate.py -> pass; rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test runtime_math_admission_gate_test -> PASS (3/3).","created_at":"2026-02-15T17:41:49Z"},{"id":1141,"issue_id":"bd-rqn","author":"AzureBluff","text":"Progress update (AzureBluff, 2026-02-19): completed production-kernel partition and governance artifact alignment to unblock bd-3ot.1.

Implemented:
- tests/runtime_math/production_kernel_manifest.v1.json: moved to minimal production set (25 modules) and explicit research_only_modules (44 modules).
- tests/conformance/math_production_set_policy.v1.json: updated change_gate.manifest_sha256 to b47cd1f6283dc3bb151fb0c57b7f8ef5c1b1afe526cf2b333d92eecc37ef257b, module_count=25, and summary counts to production-only.
- tests/conformance/math_retirement_policy.json: reflected completed migration state (RC-1=0, no active waivers, migration waves=0, summary updated).
- tests/runtime_math/runtime_math_classification_matrix.v1.json: synchronized per-module in_production_manifest / in_research_only_manifest flags and summary counts with new manifest partition.
- Re-ran scripts/runtime_math_admission_gate.py to regenerate:
  - tests/runtime_math/admission_gate_report.v1.json
  - tests/runtime_math/controller_manifest.v1.json

Gate/script verification (PASS):
- scripts/check_runtime_math_manifest.sh
- scripts/check_runtime_math_classification_matrix.sh
- scripts/check_math_production_set_policy.sh
- scripts/check_math_retirement.sh
- scripts/check_runtime_math_admission.sh
- scripts/check_math_governance.sh

RCH validation:
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test runtime_math_admission_gate_test (PASS 4/4)
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test runtime_math_classification_matrix_test (PASS 8/8)
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test math_production_set_policy_test (PASS 4/4)
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test math_retirement_test (PASS 9/9)
- rch exec -- cargo fmt --check (PASS)
- rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets (PASS)
- rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (PASS)
- rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets started remotely and progressed through broad suite but stalled in long pthread lifecycle tests; process was terminated to avoid indefinite hang.

This resolves bd-rqn’s outstanding blocker note: production manifest is now minimal, research-only routing is explicit, and policy artifacts are auditable + reproducible.
","created_at":"2026-02-19T16:39:47Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-rth1","title":"bd-yos subtask: TLS handoff and teardown correctness for spawned threads","description":"Background:
- TLS handoff correctness is required for thread-local errno and future pthread_key destructor behavior.

Goal:
- Implement and verify TLS initialization/teardown handoff for spawned threads.

Deliverables:
1) TLS init protocol during thread start.
2) Per-thread errno/TLS storage linkage checks.
3) Teardown sequencing and cleanup invariants.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Thread-local values remain isolated and stable under concurrent workloads.
- TLS teardown leaves no leaked state.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for TLS setup/teardown and isolation.
- E2E concurrent tests validating cross-thread isolation.
- Structured logs with trace_id, thread_id, tls_event, key_count, cleanup_status.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:52.128037997Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T06:12:33.353261867Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T06:12:33.353242521Z","close_reason":"Completed TLS handoff/teardown correctness fixes and verification evidence posted in comments.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","implementation","pthread","testing","tls"],"comments":[{"id":1142,"issue_id":"bd-rth1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Codex claiming this now. Found a concrete TLS table correctness bug: removing one TID currently breaks linear-probe lookup chains for colliding TIDs, which can corrupt per-thread TLS lookup after teardown. I am implementing tombstone-based delete + collision tests now.","created_at":"2026-02-22T05:19:32Z"},{"id":1143,"issue_id":"bd-rth1","author":"Codex","text":"Completed TLS handoff/teardown hardening and verification.

Implemented:
- Fixed TLS probe-chain deletion correctness in  (tombstone semantics preserve lookup chains).
- Replaced shared fallback TSD with per-thread fallback () to prevent cross-thread leakage for unregistered threads.
- Added ABI integration tests in :
  - 
  - 
  - shared guard + native-threading force helper for deterministic setup.

[31m-    let total_size = GUARD_PAGE_SIZE.checked_add(stack_size).ok_or(crate::errno::ENOMEM)?;
(B[m[32m+    let total_size = GUARD_PAGE_SIZE
(B[m[32m+        .checked_add(stack_size)
(B[m[32m+        .ok_or(crate::errno::ENOMEM)?;
(B[m 
     // Allocate the full region as read+write.
     // SAFETY: anonymous mmap with no fd, valid parameters. ✅
-  ✅
-  ✅

Note: full workspace test sweeps can still hit long-running/flaky hangs in pthread lifecycle/TSD binaries under concurrent workspace load; isolated bead-scope tests above pass consistently.","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:12:14Z"},{"id":1144,"issue_id":"bd-rth1","author":"Codex","text":"Completion summary (clean):

- Fixed TLS probe-chain deletion correctness in `crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/tls.rs` (tombstone semantics preserve collision-chain lookups).
- Replaced shared fallback TSD with per-thread fallback (`thread_local!`) to prevent cross-thread leakage for unregistered threads.

-{"id":"bd-rxgs9","title":"PORT: funopen (BSD callback-based stdio over fopencookie)","description":"BSD funopen(cookie, readfn, writefn, seekfn, closefn): callback-based custom stdio stream, the BSD analog of glibc fopencookie. Different conventions: read/write fns return int (truncated ssize_t), take int byte count; seek fn takes/returns fpos_t (off_t = i64) directly instead of via *offset pointer; mode is inferred from which callbacks are NULL (readfn+writefn=r+, readfn only=r, writefn only=w; both NULL=EINVAL). Implement by allocating a heap trampoline holding the BSD callbacks + original cookie, building a CookieIoFuncs that adapts each call (clamp size to INT_MAX, route seek through *offset), passing the trampoline pointer as the fopencookie cookie, and freeing the trampoline in the close trampoline regardless of whether user closefn succeeded.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T14:28:23.613184776Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T14:28:23.613184776Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Different conventions: read/write fns return int (truncated ssize_t), take int byte count; seek fn takes/returns fpos_t (off_t = i64) directly instead of via *offset pointer; mode is inferred from which callbacks are NULL (readfn+writefn=r+, readfn only=r, writefn only=w; both NULL=EINVAL). Implement by allocating a heap trampoline holding the BSD callbacks + original cookie, building a CookieIoFuncs that adapts each call (clamp size to INT_MAX, route seek through *offset), passing the trampoline pointer as the fopencookie cookie, and freeing the trampoline in the close trampoline regardless of whether user closefn succeeded.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T14:28:23.613184776Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T14:33:56.068090225Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T14:33:56.066031356Z","close_reason":"shipped: funopen via fopencookie trampoline + 3 abi tests including round-trip, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ryp2g","title":"_IO_adjust_column / _IO_adjust_wcolumn diverge from glibc — wrong column-counter algorithm","description":"## Defect

Both `_IO_adjust_column` (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/io_internal_abi.rs:2055`)
and `_IO_adjust_wcolumn` (line 2091) implement their own tab-aware
column tracker:

```rust
for i in 0..count as usize {
    let byte = ...;
    match byte {
        b'\n' | b'\r' => c = 0,
        b'\t' => c = (c + 8) & !7,
        _ => c += 1,
    }
}
```

The actual glibc implementation (sunrpc-adjacent — `libio/genops.c`) is:

```c
int _IO_adjust_column (unsigned start, const char *line, int count) {
    const char *ptr = line + count;
    while (ptr > line)
        if (*--ptr == '\n')
            return line + count - ptr - 1;
    return start + count;
}
```

The semantics is: **find the position past the last newline in the
buffer, OR add `count` to `start` if no newline.** Tabs are NOT
expanded; carriage returns are NOT zeroed; the function never iterates
forward through every byte.

This is an ABI behavioral divergence. Any consumer (e.g. older glibc-
linked C++ stream code, certain pretty-printers in third-party
libraries) that calls `_IO_adjust_column` to know "how far am I from
the last newline" gets a different answer from us than from real
glibc, especially when the buffer contains tabs or carriage returns.

Additional smaller defects in the current code:
- `c + 8` and `c += 1` arithmetic on `c: u32` panics in debug mode
  for adversarial column values near `u32::MAX` (theoretical only —
  caller would need to seed `col` near INT_MAX which is implausible).
- `c as c_int` at the end can return a negative result when `c >
  i32::MAX`.

## Fix

Rewrite both helpers to mirror glibc's "scan back from end for last
newline" algorithm. Keep the membrane policy gate intact. The wide
variant scans backward over `i32` wide-chars looking for `0x0A`.

## Severity

[HIGH] (P2). Pure correctness divergence — no memory-safety
exploit, but breaks ABI compatibility for third-party libraries that
depend on the documented glibc semantics. Pretty-printer column
counters, indent trackers, and any consumer that mixes
_IO_adjust_column results with raw text-position arithmetic will
silently produce wrong layouts.","notes":"Fixed in fb6e5ed2: _IO_adjust_column and _IO_adjust_wcolumn rewritten to match glibc libio/genops.c — backward scan for last newline, returns either (count - newline_pos - 1) or (start + count). Tabs and CR no longer special-cased. Saturating arithmetic eliminates the debug-mode overflow panics in the old tab-expansion path. 6 regression tests pin glibc semantics. Verified 52/52 io_internal_native_file_test pass + clippy clean.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T01:09:59.243184856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T01:15:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T01:15:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-rz0i","title":"syscall: process_madvise invalid flags should override bad pidfd and missing iov","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL when process_madvise receives invalid flags, even when the pidfd is invalid or the iovec pointer is NULL with a nonzero vlen.  {"id":"bd-rzk00","title":"PORT: __getppid + __gettid + __getuid/euid/gid/egid + __setuid/gid/euid/egid + __setsid + __setpgrp (glibc reserved aliases)","description":"Port the 12 glibc reserved-namespace aliases for process-id and credentials entry points: __getppid, __gettid, __getuid, __geteuid, __getgid, __getegid, __setuid, __setgid, __seteuid, __setegid, __setsid, __setpgrp. glibc emits these as both internal-use entry points and as the symbols some statically-linked NSS modules + libpthread internals link against. Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" wrappers delegating to the public counterparts in unistd_abi (process_abi for setpgrp on some glibcs). Tests verify each alias agrees with its public counterpart on the current process state; mutator aliases (setuid/setgid/etc) are tested only for their EPERM-on-non-root error path so they dont break the test environment.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T13:15:03.639884955Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T13:19:30.087613347Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T13:19:30.085440627Z","close_reason":"shipped: 12 glibc reserved-namespace pid/cred aliases (__getppid + __gettid + __getuid/euid/gid/egid + __setuid/gid/euid/egid + __setsid + __setpgrp) + 6 abi tests, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
